application: Group related elements by reducing whitespace between them
example: Grouping NSE C and C+ together in a bar chart

application: Use consistent color for same meaning across all visualizations
Use consistent shape/pattern for same data type
example: All percentages use bar height convention

application: Guide eye with visual flow (typically top-left to bottom-right)
svg_implementation: sort: descending by value; smooth transitions

application: Connect related elements with lines or implied paths
svg_implementation: path: smooth curves between data points

application: Humans complete incomplete patterns - use subtle backgrounds
example: Faint grid lines help complete implicit boxes

application: Use negative space to imply grouping
example: Larger gaps between categories suggest separation

application: Make primary insight pop with color saturation and size
De-emphasize supporting data with lower saturation

example: Diverging bar symmetric around center axis

application: Ensure main data stands out from background
example: Data bars contrasting with light background

application: Use layering to create visual hierarchy
example: Grid lines behind bars; bars in front; annotations in front
